title: Base Editing: CRISPR Without the Double Cut
script_summary: Explores the revolutionary potential of base editing in gene therapy.

segments.voiceover: What if you could edit a single letter in your DNA without a double cut?
Introducing base editing—a genius tool that rewrites genetic code, one letter at a time.
Unlike classic CRISPR, which cuts both DNA strands, base editing avoids that damage.
Developed in the Liu lab, this method shows promise in precise gene therapy, still in trial stages.
Imagine curing genetic diseases by simply changing one DNA letter.
Curious about the future of gene editing? Hit that follow button for more!
